task_completion.description: Open the underexposed image in GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: GIMP is open and the image 'underexposed_landscape.jpg' is loaded in the workspace. The image should initially appear very dark.
The 'Brightness-Contrast' dialog box is visible on the screen (typically accessed via the Colors menu).

task_completion.description: Adjust the brightness and contrast sliders
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Brightness and Contrast sliders in the dialog box are moved to the right (positive values). The image preview should show a noticeable improvement in exposure.

task_completion.description: Apply the changes to the image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Brightness-Contrast dialog is closed (OK was clicked) and the main GIMP canvas displays the image with significantly improved brightness and contrast compared to the original state.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical interface to modify the image
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP's menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick) being used to alter the image.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the Brightness-Contrast tool
integrity.visual_evidence: The Brightness-Contrast dialog is seen being used, rather than the agent downloading a pre-edited image or using unrelated tools to fake the result.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image is 'underexposed_landscape.jpg' located on the Desktop. The agent must use GIMP to improve its exposure. The target is to increase brightness (by 5-50 units) and enhance contrast, while avoiding excessive clipping (<2% of pixels at extreme white/black values). The agent does not need to manually export the file, as a post-task script automatically exports the active GIMP canvas to 'enhanced_landscape.jpg'. The final visual state should simply show the improved image open in GIMP.
